(Enough for 2 plates) Bring DMSO and Amplex Red reagent to room temp. Just prior to use dissolve 1 vial (.26mg) of 20mM Amplex Red reagent in 100µL of DMSO. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5f1uc","text":"Store stock solution an –20ºC, protected from light. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":54}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c2hfq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7oqiu","text":"HRP (Horseradish peroxidase) 100U/mL: Combine 15µL of 200U/mL HRP stock solution with 15µL of 1X Reaction Buffer. Store frozen at –20ºC. Reagent supplied with kit is 20U. Dissolve content with 200µL 1X Reaction buffer and divide into 22µL aliquots. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":28},{"style":"bold","offset":137,"length":112}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"40cp9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3lse","text":"20mM H²O²: (Make fresh each time.) Dilute (check bottle for %) 17.9µL H²O² (3.8%)in 982.1µL dH²O. (Check label for exact concentration) (23µL 3% H²O² into 977µL dH²O) Use promptly. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":34},{"style":"sub","offset":6,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":8,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":71,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":73,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":94,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":146,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":148,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":163,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3f9sg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7aud1","text":"Catalase: Prepare a 1000U/mL stock. Reagent supplied with kit is 100U. Dissolve vial in 100µL dH²O. Aliquot and store at –20ºC. Make 10U/mL with 1µL 1000U/mL stock into 99µL dH²O. Make 1U/ml with 10µL 10U/ml into 90µL dH²O.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10},{"style":"bold","offset":133,"length":6},{"style":"bold","offset":184,"length":6},{"style":"sub","offset":96,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":176,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":220,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":203406,"guid":"41AB9F98341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Amplex Red Catalase Assay Kit ","mol_weight":0,"sku":"A-22180","url":"https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/A22180?SID=srch-srp-A22180","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":679,"name":"Molecular Probes","link":"https://www.thermofisher.com/us/en/home/brands/molecular-probes.html","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: 1. Homogenate tissue in 1X Reaction Buffer on ice . 2. Using a black plate, pipette 25µL of diluted standards, controls (if any) and samples into wells. (Final concentration will be fourfold lower, 0 to 10µM) 3. Prepare stock solution of 20mM H ² O ² then prepare a 40µM H ² O ² dilution by adding 10µl of 20mM H ² O ² to 4.99mL 1X Reaction Buffer. 4. Pipet 25µL of 40µM H ² O ² solution into each well. 5. Incubate for 30 minutes at room temp. 6. Prepare stock solution of 10mM Amplex Red reagent and divide into 50µL aliquots and freeze immediately. 7. Prepare stock solution of 100U/ml HRP and divide into 20mL aliquots. 8. Prepare 100µM Amplex Red reagent containing 0.4U/mL HRP by adding 50µL of 10mM Amplex Red stock solution and 20µL of 100U/ml HRP stock solution to 4.93mL 1X Reaction Buffer. 9. Begin 2 nd phase of reaction by adding 50µL of the above to each well. 10. Place plate into Fluroskan holder and click "START" . 11. Take 4 readings @ 15 minute intervals using 544/590 filter pairs. (Generally take 3 reading which would be after 30 min. incubation as recommended.) 12. Save raw data as an Excel file into the CTx data folder. Use the naming convention CTXXXX.xls, where XXXX is the date in mmdd format. 13. Select Process>Organize. Choose the appropriate data to organize (usually Measure1), then click "OK" . This re-arranges the data into columns. 14. Save organized data as an Excel file into the Catalase data folder. Use the naming convention ctXXXXor.xls, where XXXX is the date in mmdd format.
creator: Lili Liang
